steps.title: Check Table A
steps.content:
Substitute the values from table A into the inequality $y > 13x - 18$:
  • For x = 3, y = 21: $$21 > 13(3) - 18 \Rightarrow 21 > 21$$ This does not hold since it should be strictly greater than (not equal to).

steps.title: Check Table B
steps.content:
Substitute the values from table B into the inequality $y > 13x - 18$:
  • For x = 5, y = 42: $$42 > 13(5) - 18 \Rightarrow 42 > 47$$ This does not hold.

steps.title: Check Table C
steps.content:
Substitute the values from table C into the inequality $y > 13x - 18$:
  • For x = 8, y = 81: $$81 > 13(8) - 18 \Rightarrow 81 > 70$$ This holds but it's worth checking all values.

steps.title: Check Table D
steps.content:
Substitute the values from table D into the inequality $y > 13x - 18$:
  • For x = 3, y = 26: $$26 > 13(3) - 18 \Rightarrow 26 > 21$$ This holds.
All provided values in table D satisfy the inequality.
